what_it_does: Registers a metadata-only role-mind named 'extractor' that points to the existing role 'roles/mine_exchange' as the fast structured-fact extraction leg of a larger composition.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring and a MIND dictionary.
MIND has keys: id, kind, role, desc.
It explicitly references 'roles/mine_exchange' as the underlying role it binds.
It self-labels as the EXTRACT half of an extraction-vs-judgment pair.
There are no imports, functions, classes, or executable code.
The docstring states 'reuse-don't-parallel: a mind is a named reference to a real role.'
standouts: The entire file is a named pointer/intent declaration rather than an implementation.
It frames itself as one leg of an N+1 composition (extractor vs judge).
It binds an existing role by string reference rather than duplicating logic.
conventions: Uses an all-caps MIND constant as a registry-style metadata object.
Module docstring explains architectural intent before data.
Metadata-driven role definition with id/kind/role/desc fields.
concerns: The binding to 'roles/mine_exchange' is only a string reference with no local validation.
No schema shown for the 'structured facts' it claims to emit.
No code enforces that this mind actually behaves as described.
notes: A thin registry entry that gives the existing mine_exchange extraction role a composable identity called extractor.
questions: Where is roles/mine_exchange defined and how is the binding resolved at runtime?
How are role-minds loaded and registered by the composition layer?
What is the exact shape of the structured facts emitted for the judge?
What does the full N+1 composition look like and how is the judge role specified?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to bind roles/mine_exchange as the extract leg; actual content is a static MIND metadata dict, so the binding is declarative/by-reference rather than implemented here.
intention_signals: Create a lightweight, documented, named slot for an existing extraction role so the larger system can compose it as the fast fact-extractor leg without reimplementing that role.
novelty: The file is essentially a self-documenting role alias whose main content is a design rationale about reuse-don't-parallel.
contribution: A stable named identity and intent description for the fact-extractor leg, giving the composition layer a referenceable, documented mind slot that delegates to an existing role.
summary_for_embedding: company/minds/extractor.py registers a metadata-only role-mind named extractor that points to roles/mine_exchange as the fast structured-fact extraction leg of an N+1 mind composition.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable named reference to the existing mine_exchange extraction role so the larger system can compose it as the extract leg alongside a judge.
